description_long: This is a medium-sized wooden barrel with a cylindrical shape, featuring three prominent, slightly weathered metallic hoops encircling its body for structural reinforcement. The wood staves are vertically aligned, showing visible grain and texture, with a slightly uneven, hand-crafted appearance. The top of the barrel is closed with a wooden lid, which has a small, round stone or cork bung set into its center, likely for filling or emptying contents. The metallic hoops are painted or coated in a dull, grayish-white color, contrasting with the natural brown tones of the wood. There are no visible handles or external markings, giving the barrel a utilitarian, rustic look. The overall finish is matte and rough, suggesting it is designed for practical use in urban or outdoor environments such as alleys, courtyards, or storage areas.
description: A rustic wooden barrel with three grayish metal hoops and a central stone or cork bung on its wooden lid, featuring a rough, matte finish.
description_view_0: The barrel is seen from a slightly elevated angle, showing the top lid with a central stone or cork bung and the full curvature of the wooden staves and metal hoops.
description_view_1: This view highlights the side of the barrel, with the bung positioned closer to the edge of the lid and the texture of the wood and hoops more visible.
description_view_2: The barrel is shown with the bung centered on the lid, emphasizing the symmetry of the hoops and the vertical wood grain.
description_view_3: The barrel is rotated to display the bung near the lid's edge, with the metallic hoops and wood texture clearly visible along the side.
